behavior_summary: When the player 'looks' at the ALTAR, the object's U8P_ALTAR::look handler emits a single short bark string "altar " (note trailing space) and then suspends the process.
The script does not read or write any global flags, call external functions, present menus, or branch on conditions — it is a minimal descriptive handler.
The presence of Unknown nodes in the raw suggests simple process delimiters; the effective behavior is: display description -> suspend/end.
notable_control_flow: Entry via a process-type U8P_ALTAR::look handler (processType 0x0208) for the ALTAR triggers the routine immediately.
Linear flow: bark output followed by a suspend/yield; no branching or looping is present.
No side effects (no flag writes, no item manipulation, no other process spawns).
open_questions: Why the bark string contains a trailing space ("altar ") — likely insignificant formatting or leftover from source data (low confidence).
Raw Unknown nodes are present (e.g., '{' and 'suspend') — their exact representation in the original script is inferred rather than certain.

main_function.name: U8P_ALTAR::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description shown to the player when examining the ALTAR and then pause/terminate the U8P_ALTAR::look interaction.
entry_conditions: Triggered when the player uses the 'U8P_ALTAR::look' verb on the ALTAR (processType 0x0208).
Inferred: also triggers when an actor enters the object's U8P_ALTAR::look/examine fast area or uses the engine's inspect action.

function_map.name: U8P_ALTAR::look
function_map.role: examine/U8P_ALTAR::look handler that emits a single descriptive bark and suspends the process
